options:

  -fileHandler <handler>
                    override the fileHandler

                    pre-load libraries

  -noFunctionObjects
                    do not execute functionObjects
  -region <name>    specify alternative mesh region
  -srcDoc           display source code in browser
  -doc              display application documentation in browser
  -help             print the usage

Block description

  For a given block, the correspondence between the ordering of
  vertex labels and face labels is shown below.
  For vertex numbering in the sequence 0 to 7 (block, centre):
    faces 0 (f0) and 1 are left and right, respectively;
    faces 2 and 3 are front and back; 
    and faces 4 and 5 are bottom and top::

                 7 ---- 6
            f5   |\     |\   f3
            |    | 4 ---- 5   \
            |    3 |--- 2 |    \
            |     \|     \|    f2
            f4     0 ---- 1

       Z         f0 ----- f1
       |  Y
       | /
       O --- X

{

    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    (0 0 0)
    (0.02 0 0)
    (0.02 0.02 0)
    (0 0.02 0)
    (0 0 0.001)
    (0.02 0 0.001)
    (0.02 0.02 0.001)
    (0 0.02 0.001)
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    air
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,K)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='chemistryProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile

Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>
Please review the error logs and provide guidance on how to resolve the reported errors. Make sure your suggestions adhere to user requirements and do not contradict it.
response:
## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.</review_analysis>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.</reviewer_analysis>

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.

response:
## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means that the `physicalProperties` file does not contain entries for the species `CH4`, `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, and `CO2`. The `reactingFoam` solver reads the species list from `physicalProperties` to know which species are present and their thermodynamic properties.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file contains the species list (`CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2`) and the `reactions` dictionary, but it is missing the individual species sub-dictionaries (the `CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, etc. blocks) that `reactingFoam` expects. The `thermo.compressibleGas` file has these species sub-dictionaries, but `physicalProperties` does not.

## Proposed Solution

**Add the individual species sub-dictionaries to `constant/physicalProperties`**. The file needs to include the `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` line followed by the individual species blocks with their thermodynamic data (molecular weight, Cp coefficients, transport properties, etc.) for each species.

The `physicalProperties` file should have entries for all five species exactly as they appear in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`. The species sub-dictionaries should include:
- `CH4 { ... }` with molWeight, thermodynamics (Tlow, Thigh, Tcommon, highCpCoeffs, lowCpCoeffs), and transport (As, Ts)
- `O2 { ... }` with the same structure
- `N2 { ... }` with the same structure  
- `H2O { ... }` with the same structure
- `CO2 { ... }` with the same structure

These are already present in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` — they just need to be copied into `constant/physicalProperties`. The `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` and `chemistryReader` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas` should remain as-is.

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Add the five species sub-dictionaries (`CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, `N2 { ... }`, `H2O { ... }`, `CO2 { ... }`) with their thermodynamic and transport data. The species list line `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` is already present, so only the individual species blocks need to be added.

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means that the `physicalProperties` file does not contain entries for the species `CH4`, `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, and `CO2`. The `reactingFoam` solver reads the species list from `physicalProperties` to know which species are present and their thermodynamic properties.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file contains the species list (`CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2`) and the `reactions` dictionary, but it is missing the individual species sub-dictionaries (the `CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, etc. blocks) that `reactingFoam` expects. The `thermo.compressibleGas` file has these species sub-dictionaries, but `physicalProperties` does not.

## Proposed Solution

**Add the individual species sub-dictionaries to `constant/physicalProperties`**. The file needs to include the `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` line followed by the individual species blocks with their thermodynamic data (molecular weight, Cp coefficients, transport properties, etc.) for each species.

The `physicalProperties` file should have entries for all five species exactly as they appear in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`. The species sub-dictionaries should include:
- `CH4 { ... }` with molWeight, thermodynamics (Tlow, Thigh, Tcommon, highCpCoeffs, lowCpCoeffs), and transport (As, Ts)
- `O2 { ... }` with the same structure
- `N2 { ... }` with the same structure  
- `H2O { ... }` with the same structure
- `CO2 { ... }` with the same structure

These are already present in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` — they just need to be copied into `constant/physicalProperties`. The `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` and `chemistryReader` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas` should remain as-is.

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Add the five species sub-dictionaries (`CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, `N2 { ... }`, `H2O { ... }`, `CO2 { ... }`) with their thermodynamic and transport data. The species list line `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` is already present, so only the individual species blocks need to be added.</review_analysis>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means that the `physicalProperties` file does not contain entries for the species `CH4`, `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, and `CO2`. The `reactingFoam` solver reads the species list from `physicalProperties` to know which species are present and their thermodynamic properties.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file contains the species list (`CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2`) and the `reactions` dictionary, but it is missing the individual species sub-dictionaries (the `CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, etc. blocks) that `reactingFoam` expects. The `thermo.compressibleGas` file has these species sub-dictionaries, but `physicalProperties` does not.

## Proposed Solution

**Add the individual species sub-dictionaries to `constant/physicalProperties`**. The file needs to include the `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` line followed by the individual species blocks with their thermodynamic data (molecular weight, Cp coefficients, transport properties, etc.) for each species.

The `physicalProperties` file should have entries for all five species exactly as they appear in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`. The species sub-dictionaries should include:
- `CH4 { ... }` with molWeight, thermodynamics (Tlow, Thigh, Tcommon, highCpCoeffs, lowCpCoeffs), and transport (As, Ts)
- `O2 { ... }` with the same structure
- `N2 { ... }` with the same structure  
- `H2O { ... }` with the same structure
- `CO2 { ... }` with the same structure

These are already present in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` — they just need to be copied into `constant/physicalProperties`. The `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` and `chemistryReader` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas` should remain as-is.

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Add the five species sub-dictionaries (`CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, `N2 { ... }`, `H2O { ... }`, `CO2 { ... }`) with their thermodynamic and transport data. The species list line `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` is already present, so only the individual species blocks need to be added.</reviewer_analysis>

    "rho.*"
    {

    }

    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;

        relTol          0.1;
    }

    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {

        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"

        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

{

    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;

response:
## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword species is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means that the `physicalProperties` file does not contain a `species` keyword. The `reactingFoam` solver reads the list of species from `physicalProperties` to know which species are present in the simulation. The `species` keyword should list all species names (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2) that are defined in the file.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file contains the species list in the `thermoType` dictionary (it has `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` inside the `thermoType` block), but the `species` keyword at the top level of the file is missing. The `reactingFoam` solver looks for a top-level `species` entry in `physicalProperties`, not one nested inside `thermoType`.

## Proposed Solution

**Add a top-level `species` keyword to `constant/physicalProperties`** that lists all species. The file should have:

```
species
(
    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2
);
```

This should be placed at the top level of the file, outside of the `thermoType` dictionary. The `thermoType` dictionary can keep its internal `species` list, but the top-level `species` entry is what `reactingFoam` requires.

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Add a top-level `species` entry listing all five species: `CH4`, `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, and `CO2`. This should be placed before the `thermoType` dictionary or after it — the key point is that it must be a top-level keyword (not nested inside another dictionary).

The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already has the correct species sub-dictionaries and the `foamChemistryFile` entry, so no changes are needed there. The `chemistryProperties` file already has `chemistry on;` and the `reactions` dictionary, so no changes are needed there either.

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword species is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means that the `physicalProperties` file does not contain a `species` keyword. The `reactingFoam` solver reads the list of species from `physicalProperties` to know which species are present in the simulation. The `species` keyword should list all species names (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2) that are defined in the file.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file contains the species list in the `thermoType` dictionary (it has `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` inside the `thermoType` block), but the `species` keyword at the top level of the file is missing. The `reactingFoam` solver looks for a top-level `species` entry in `physicalProperties`, not one nested inside `thermoType`.

## Proposed Solution

**Add a top-level `species` keyword to `constant/physicalProperties`** that lists all species. The file should have:

```
species
(
    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2
);
```

This should be placed at the top level of the file, outside of the `thermoType` dictionary. The `thermoType` dictionary can keep its internal `species` list, but the top-level `species` entry is what `reactingFoam` requires.

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Add a top-level `species` entry listing all five species: `CH4`, `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, and `CO2`. This should be placed before the `thermoType` dictionary or after it — the key point is that it must be a top-level keyword (not nested inside another dictionary).

The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already has the correct species sub-dictionaries and the `foamChemistryFile` entry, so no changes are needed there. The `chemistryProperties` file already has `chemistry on;` and the `reactions` dictionary, so no changes are needed there either.</review_analysis>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword species is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means that the `physicalProperties` file does not contain a `species` keyword. The `reactingFoam` solver reads the list of species from `physicalProperties` to know which species are present in the simulation. The `species` keyword should list all species names (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2) that are defined in the file.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file contains the species list in the `thermoType` dictionary (it has `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` inside the `thermoType` block), but the `species` keyword at the top level of the file is missing. The `reactingFoam` solver looks for a top-level `species` entry in `physicalProperties`, not one nested inside `thermoType`.

## Proposed Solution

**Add a top-level `species` keyword to `constant/physicalProperties`** that lists all species. The file should have:

```
species
(
    CH4
    O2
    N2
    H2O
    CO2
);
```

This should be placed at the top level of the file, outside of the `thermoType` dictionary. The `thermoType` dictionary can keep its internal `species` list, but the top-level `species` entry is what `reactingFoam` requires.

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Add a top-level `species` entry listing all five species: `CH4`, `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, and `CO2`. This should be placed before the `thermoType` dictionary or after it — the key point is that it must be a top-level keyword (not nested inside another dictionary).

The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already has the correct species sub-dictionaries and the `foamChemistryFile` entry, so no changes are needed there. The `chemistryProperties` file already has `chemistry on;` and the `reactions` dictionary, so no changes are needed there either.</reviewer_analysis>

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.
</Review_Analysis>

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This is the same error as Attempt 2, but now the `species` keyword is present in `physicalProperties`. The `reactingFoam` solver is looking for a `CH4` sub-dictionary within `physicalProperties`, but it cannot find it.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file has the top-level `species` list, but it is missing the individual species sub-dictionaries (`CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, etc.). The `reactingFoam` solver reads the species list from `physicalProperties` and then looks up each species name as a sub-dictionary to get their thermodynamic and transport properties. The `thermo.compressibleGas` file contains these species sub-dictionaries, but `physicalProperties` does not.

## Proposed Solution

**Add the individual species sub-dictionaries to `constant/physicalProperties`**. The file needs to include the `CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, `N2 { ... }`, `H2O { ... }`, and `CO2 { ... }` blocks with their thermodynamic data (molecular weight, Cp coefficients, transport properties, etc.) for each species.

The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already contains these species sub-dictionaries with all the required data. Copy those exact blocks from `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` into `constant/physicalProperties`. The `physicalProperties` file should have:

1. The top-level `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` line (already present)
2. The `thermoType` dictionary (already present)
3. The individual species sub-dictionaries for all five species

The `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` and `chemistryReader` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas` should remain as-is. The `chemistryProperties` file is already correct with `chemistry on;` and the `reactions` dictionary.

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Add the five species sub-dictionaries (`CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, `N2 { ... }`, `H2O { ... }`, `CO2 { ... }`) with their thermodynamic and transport data. These should be copied directly from `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` — they are already present there and just need to be added to `physicalProperties`.</review_analysis>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary "constant/physicalProperties"
```

This is the same error as Attempt 2, but now the `species` keyword is present in `physicalProperties`. The `reactingFoam` solver is looking for a `CH4` sub-dictionary within `physicalProperties`, but it cannot find it.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file has the top-level `species` list, but it is missing the individual species sub-dictionaries (`CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, etc.). The `reactingFoam` solver reads the species list from `physicalProperties` and then looks up each species name as a sub-dictionary to get their thermodynamic and transport properties. The `thermo.compressibleGas` file contains these species sub-dictionaries, but `physicalProperties` does not.

## Proposed Solution

**Add the individual species sub-dictionaries to `constant/physicalProperties`**. The file needs to include the `CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, `N2 { ... }`, `H2O { ... }`, and `CO2 { ... }` blocks with their thermodynamic data (molecular weight, Cp coefficients, transport properties, etc.) for each species.

The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already contains these species sub-dictionaries with all the required data. Copy those exact blocks from `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` into `constant/physicalProperties`. The `physicalProperties` file should have:

1. The top-level `species (CH4 O2 N2 H2O CO2);` line (already present)
2. The `thermoType` dictionary (already present)
3. The individual species sub-dictionaries for all five species

The `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` and `chemistryReader` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas` should remain as-is. The `chemistryProperties` file is already correct with `chemistry on;` and the `reactions` dictionary.

## Summary of Changes

<user_requirement>do a counterflow flame simulation using OpenFOAM reactingFoam solver, employing a compressible flow solver for detailed combustion modeling. Define the computational domain as a 2D rectangular region with a width of 0.02 meters and a height of 0.02 meters. Use CH4, O2 and N2 as species. Include chemical kinetics using the reaction mechanism. velocity of fuel is 0.1 m/s and velocity of air is -0.1 m/s. Set the simulation runtime from 0 to 0.5 seconds with a write interval of 0.05 seconds and deltaT of 1e-6.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.

You will be provided with a similar case reference, which is a list of similar cases that are ordered by similarity. You can use this reference to help you understand the user requirement and the error.When an error indicates that a specific keyword is undefined (for example, 'div(phi,(p|rho)) is undefined'), your response must propose a solution that simply defines that exact keyword as shown in the error log. Do not reinterpret or modify the keyword (e.g., do not treat '|' as 'or'); instead, assume it is meant to be taken literally. Propose ideas on how to resolve the errors, but do not modify any files directly. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. Do not ask the user any questions.The user will supply all relevant foam files along with the error logs, and within the logs, you will find both the error content and the corresponding error command indicated by the log file name.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,K)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.
</Review_Analysis>

The error is:

```

This is the same error as Attempts 2, 3, and 4. The `physicalProperties` file still does not contain the `CH4` sub-dictionary. Previous attempts tried adding the species sub-dictionaries to `physicalProperties`, but the error persists.

## Root Cause Analysis

Looking more carefully at the `physicalProperties` file, I notice that the `species` list is present, but the individual species sub-dictionaries are **not** properly formatted or are missing entirely. The error message specifically says `CH4` is undefined, meaning the solver is looking for a sub-dictionary named `CH4` within `physicalProperties`.

However, there's a more fundamental issue: **`physicalProperties` should not contain the species sub-dictionaries at all**. In OpenFOAM 10, the `physicalProperties` file should only contain the `species` list and the `thermoType` dictionary. The individual species thermodynamic data should be in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`, and `physicalProperties` should reference that file via `chemistryReader`.

The real problem is that the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` is not correctly set up to read the chemistry data from `thermo.compressibleGas`. The `chemistryReader` needs to be configured to read from the correct file, and the `thermo.compressibleGas` file needs to expose the chemistry data in a way that `chemistryReader` can access it.

## Proposed Solution

**Fix the `chemistryReader` configuration in `constant/physicalProperties`** to properly read the chemistry data from `thermo.compressibleGas`. The current `physicalProperties` file has:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

This is correct, but the `thermo.compressibleGas` file needs to have the chemistry data accessible via the `foamChemistryReader`. Looking at the `thermo.compressibleGas` file, it already has:

```

foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";
```

The issue is that `physicalProperties` needs to reference the same chemistry file. The `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the chemistry data that `thermo.compressibleGas` provides.

```

```

This is already present. The real issue is that the `physicalProperties` file needs to have the `species` list at the top level (which it does), and the `thermoType` dictionary (which it does), but it also needs to have the `chemistryReader` properly configured to read from the reactions file.

The core issue is that `physicalProperties` was missing the individual species sub-dictionaries at the top level. Previous attempts added them but apparently the formatting was wrong or they were placed incorrectly. The `fuel { ... }` block is incorrect — species data should be at the top level, not nested. ## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.

## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 45 the punctuation token '/'
```

This error occurs in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` at line 45 in the `highCpCoeffs` entry for `CH4`. The error message indicates that the parser expected a scalar value but found a `/` character, which means the coefficient format is incorrect.

Looking at line 45 of `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`:

```
highCpCoeffs    ( 1.63543 0.0100842 -3.36924e-06 5.34973e-10 -5.03372e-14 -1.0e5/209285 );
```

The issue is the `/` in `-1.0e5/209285`. OpenFOAM expects all coefficients to be valid scalar values (numbers), and the `/` character is not a valid scalar token. The coefficient should be a single numeric value, not a division expression.

## Root Cause

The `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries in the species thermodynamic data contain division expressions like `-1.0e5/209285` instead of numeric values. OpenFOAM's parser reads these as tokens and expects pure scalars, not arithmetic expressions.

## Proposed Solution

**Replace all division expressions in `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` with their computed numeric values** in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`. Specifically:

1. In `CH4`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/209285` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 209285 ≈ -0.4778`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/114964` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 114964 ≈ -0.8698`

2. In `O2`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/217.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 217.76 ≈ -459.27`

3. In `N2`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/217.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 217.76 ≈ -459.27`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/161.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 161.76 ≈ -618.28`

4. In `H2O`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/209285` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 209285 ≈ -0.4778`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/209285` (in lowCpCoeffs) with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 209285 ≈ -0.4778`

5. In `CO2`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/217.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 217.76 ≈ -459.27`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/114964` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 114964 ≈ -0.8698`

wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 45 the punctuation token '/'
```

This error occurs in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` at line 45 in the `highCpCoeffs` entry for `CH4`. The error message indicates that the parser expected a scalar value but found a `/` character, which means the coefficient format is incorrect.

Looking at line 45 of `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`:

```
highCpCoeffs    ( 1.63543 0.0100842 -3.36924e-06 5.34973e-10 -5.03372e-14 -1.0e5/209285 );
```

The issue is the `/` in `-1.0e5/209285`. OpenFOAM expects all coefficients to be valid scalar values (numbers), and the `/` character is not a valid scalar token. The coefficient should be a single numeric value, not a division expression.

## Root Cause

The `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries in the species thermodynamic data contain division expressions like `-1.0e5/209285` instead of numeric values. OpenFOAM's parser reads these as tokens and expects pure scalars, not arithmetic expressions.

## Proposed Solution

**Replace all division expressions in `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` with their computed numeric values** in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`. Specifically:

1. In `CH4`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/209285` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 209285 ≈ -0.4778`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/114964` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 114964 ≈ -0.8698`

2. In `O2`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/217.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 217.76 ≈ -459.27`

3. In `N2`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/217.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 217.76 ≈ -459.27`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/161.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 161.76 ≈ -618.28`

4. In `H2O`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/209285` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 209285 ≈ -0.4778`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/209285` (in lowCpCoeffs) with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 209285 ≈ -0.4778`

5. In `CO2`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/217.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 217.76 ≈ -459.27`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/114964` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 114964 ≈ -0.8698`

The corrected `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries should contain only numeric values, not division expressions. For example, `CH4`'s `highCpCoeffs` should become:

```
highCpCoeffs    ( 1.63543 0.0100842 -3.36924e-06 5.34973e-10 -5.03372e-14 -0.4778 );
```

And `lowCpCoeffs` should become:

```
lowCpCoeffs     ( 5.14988 -0.013671 4.91801e-05 -4.84744e-08 1.66694e-11 -0.8698 );
```

All other species entries that contain division expressions should be similarly corrected.

## Summary

The only change needed is in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`: replace all division expressions (`-1.0e5/209285`, `-1.0e5/114964`, `-1.0e5/217.76`) in the `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries of all five species with their computed numeric values. This is a straightforward arithmetic fix — no other files need modification.</review_analysis>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 45 the punctuation token '/'
```

This error occurs in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` at line 45 in the `highCpCoeffs` entry for `CH4`. The error message indicates that the parser expected a scalar value but found a `/` character, which means the coefficient format is incorrect.

Looking at line 45 of `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`:

```
highCpCoeffs    ( 1.63543 0.0100842 -3.36924e-06 5.34973e-10 -5.03372e-14 -1.0e5/209285 );
```

The issue is the `/` in `-1.0e5/209285`. OpenFOAM expects all coefficients to be valid scalar values (numbers), and the `/` character is not a valid scalar token. The coefficient should be a single numeric value, not a division expression.

## Root Cause

The `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries in the species thermodynamic data contain division expressions like `-1.0e5/209285` instead of numeric values. OpenFOAM's parser reads these as tokens and expects pure scalars, not arithmetic expressions.

## Proposed Solution

**Replace all division expressions in `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` with their computed numeric values** in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`. Specifically:

1. In `CH4`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/209285` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 209285 ≈ -0.4778`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/114964` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 114964 ≈ -0.8698`

2. In `O2`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/217.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 217.76 ≈ -459.27`

3. In `N2`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/217.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 217.76 ≈ -459.27`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/161.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 161.76 ≈ -618.28`

4. In `H2O`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/209285` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 209285 ≈ -0.4778`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/209285` (in lowCpCoeffs) with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 209285 ≈ -0.4778`

5. In `CO2`:
   - Replace `-1.0e5/217.76` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 217.76 ≈ -459.27`
   - Replace `-1.0e5/114964` with the computed value: `-1.0e5 / 114964 ≈ -0.8698`

The corrected `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries should contain only numeric values, not division expressions. For example, `CH4`'s `highCpCoeffs` should become:

```
highCpCoeffs    ( 1.63543 0.0100842 -3.36924e-06 5.34973e-10 -5.03372e-14 -0.4778 );
```

And `lowCpCoeffs` should become:

```
lowCpCoeffs     ( 5.14988 -0.013671 4.91801e-05 -4.84744e-08 1.66694e-11 -0.8698 );
```

All other species entries that contain division expressions should be similarly corrected.

## Summary

The only change needed is in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`: replace all division expressions (`-1.0e5/209285`, `-1.0e5/114964`, `-1.0e5/217.76`) in the `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries of all five species with their computed numeric values. This is a straightforward arithmetic fix — no other files need modification.</reviewer_analysis>

## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.

<review_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 45 the punctuation token '/'
```

This error occurs in `constant/physicalProperties/CH4/thermodynamics/highCpCoeffs` at line 45. The error message indicates that the parser expected a scalar value but found a `/` character. This means that somewhere in the `physicalProperties` file, there is a division expression (like `value/otherValue`) that OpenFOAM cannot parse as a scalar.

Looking at the error trace, it's pointing to `constant/physicalProperties/CH4/thermodynamics/highCpCoeffs` at line 45. This file is a sub-dictionary within `physicalProperties`, and the issue is that the `highCpCoeffs` entry contains a division expression instead of a numeric value.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file contains species sub-dictionaries (CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2) with thermodynamic data. The `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries in these species sub-dictionaries contain division expressions like `-1.0e5/209285` instead of numeric values. OpenFOAM's parser reads these as tokens and expects pure scalars, not arithmetic expressions.

However, this is the **same issue** that was fixed in Attempt 6 in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`. The problem is that the `physicalProperties` file also contains these division expressions in the species sub-dictionaries, and they haven't been fixed there.

## Proposed Solution

**Fix the division expressions in `constant/physicalProperties`** — specifically, in the `CH4` sub-dictionary within `physicalProperties`, the `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries contain division expressions that need to be replaced with their computed numeric values.

lowCpCoeffs     ( 5.14988 -0.013671 4.91801e-05 -4.84744e-08 1.66694e-11 -0.8698 );

These are the same computed values from Attempt 6. The division expressions must be replaced with their actual numeric results in **all** species sub-dictionaries within `constant/physicalProperties`.

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
wrong token type - expected Scalar, found on line 45 the punctuation token '/'
```

This error occurs in `constant/physicalProperties/CH4/thermodynamics/highCpCoeffs` at line 45. The error message indicates that the parser expected a scalar value but found a `/` character. This means that somewhere in the `physicalProperties` file, there is a division expression (like `value/otherValue`) that OpenFOAM cannot parse as a scalar.

Looking at the error trace, it's pointing to `constant/physicalProperties/CH4/thermodynamics/highCpCoeffs` at line 45. This file is a sub-dictionary within `physicalProperties`, and the issue is that the `highCpCoeffs` entry contains a division expression instead of a numeric value.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file contains species sub-dictionaries (CH4, O2, N2, H2O, CO2) with thermodynamic data. The `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries in these species sub-dictionaries contain division expressions like `-1.0e5/209285` instead of numeric values. OpenFOAM's parser reads these as tokens and expects pure scalars, not arithmetic expressions.

However, this is the **same issue** that was fixed in Attempt 6 in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas`. The problem is that the `physicalProperties` file also contains these division expressions in the species sub-dictionaries, and they haven't been fixed there.

## Proposed Solution

**Fix the division expressions in `constant/physicalProperties`** — specifically, in the `CH4` sub-dictionary within `physicalProperties`, the `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries contain division expressions that need to be replaced with their computed numeric values.

        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors

    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.

    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,K)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss limitedLinear 1;

    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

}

{

}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PCG;
        preconditioner  DIC;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-08;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

The `reactingMixture` mixture type is **not supported** in this version of OpenFOAM (OpenFOAM 10). The supported mixture types are `pureMixture`, `coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture`, `multiComponentMixture`, and `valueMultiComponentMixture`.

## Root Cause

The `physicalProperties` file references `reactingMixture` as the mixture type, but this mixture type was removed/replaced in OpenFOAM 10. The `reactingMixture` type was typically used in OpenFOAM 8 and earlier versions.

## Proposed Solution

**Change the `thermoType` in `constant/physicalProperties`** from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`, which is the correct mixture type for OpenFOAM 10 when using a multi-species reacting flow.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, change:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         reactingMixture;
    ...
}
```

to:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Additionally, the `chemistryReader` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should reference the correct chemistry file. The current file references `<constant>/reactions`, but the `reactions` file is duplicated (both `constant/reactions` and `constant/reactions` exist). The `thermo.compressibleGas` file already includes the `foamChemistryFile` entry pointing to `<constant>/reactions`, so the `chemistryReader` in `physicalProperties` should reference the same file. The `chemistryReader` line in `physicalProperties` should be:

```
chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;
```

and the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `constant/thermo.compressibleGas` should point to `<constant>/reactions` (which it already does).

## Summary of Changes

1. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Change `mixture reactingMixture;` to `mixture multiComponentMixture;`
2. **`constant/thermo.compressibleGas`**: Keep `foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";` as-is (it already references the correct reactions file)
3. **`constant/physicalProperties`**: Keep `chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;` as-is (it will work with the `foamChemistryFile` entry in `thermo.compressibleGas`)

These changes align with the OpenFOAM 10 API, where `multiComponentMixture` is the appropriate mixture type for multi-species reacting flows with `hePsiThermo`.

```

This error occurs in `constant/physicalProperties/CH4/thermodynamics/highCpCoeffs` at line 45. The error message indicates that the parser expected a scalar value but found a `)` character. This means the coefficient array format is malformed — specifically, there's an extra or misplaced parenthesis.

## Root Cause Analysis

Looking at all previous attempts, the issue has persisted across 9 attempts. The problem is consistently that the `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries in the species sub-dictionaries within `physicalProperties` contain formatting issues with parentheses. The error at line 45 (the closing `)`) suggests that the array is either missing a closing parenthesis or has an extra opening parenthesis somewhere.

The current `physicalProperties` file has species sub-dictionaries added, but the formatting of the coefficient arrays appears to have an issue. The error message specifically points to line 45 in `CH4/thermodynamics/highCpCoeffs`, which is the closing parenthesis of the coefficient array.

## Proposed Solution

**Fix the formatting of the coefficient arrays in `constant/physicalProperties`**. The issue is that the `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries in the species sub-dictionaries are not properly formatted. The error at line 45 (the closing `)`) suggests that the array is missing a closing parenthesis or has an extra opening parenthesis.

Specifically, in `constant/physicalProperties`, within the `CH4 { ... }` block, the `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entries should be formatted as:

```
highCpCoeffs    ( 1.63543 0.0100842 -3.36924e-06 5.34973e-10 -5.03372e-14 -0.4778 );
lowCpCoeffs     ( 5.14988 -0.013671 4.91801e-05 -4.84744e-08 1.66694e-11 -0.8698 );
```

The key fix is ensuring that each coefficient array has exactly one opening parenthesis `(` at the beginning and exactly one closing parenthesis `);` at the end. The error at line 45 is the closing `)` of the `highCpCoeffs` array, so that parenthesis must be present and properly placed.

Check all five species sub-dictionaries (`CH4`, `O2`, `N2`, `H2O`, `CO2`) in `constant/physicalProperties` to ensure that:
1. Each `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` entry starts with `(` and ends with `);`
2. There are no extra opening parentheses or missing closing parentheses
3. All entries are properly terminated with a semicolon
4. No division expressions remain (they should have been converted to numeric values in previous attempts)

The same formatting check should be applied to all other species sub-dictionaries to ensure consistency.

## Summary

The only change needed is in `constant/physicalProperties`: verify and correct the formatting of the `highCpCoeffs` and `lowCpCoeffs` arrays in all five species sub-dictionaries.
